entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species (Sorghum bicolor) whose cultivars were studied for chemical changes during germination

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of grain sprouting over specific time intervals (16, 48, 96, and 144 h)

entities.name: DIASTATIC ACTIVITY
entities.description: The enzymatic activity in sorghum grains that varies among cultivars and changes during germination

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: The protein content in sorghum grains that shows variation during germination

entities.name: STARCH
entities.description: The starch content in sorghum grains that decreases during the germination process

entities.name: SOLUBLE SUGARS
entities.description: The soluble sugar content in sorghum grains that increases during the germination process

entities.name: TANNIN
entities.description: The tannin content in sorghum grains studied during the germination process

entities.name: TOTAL PHENOLS
entities.description: The total phenols content in sorghum grains studied during the germination process

relationships.description: Sorghum cultivars were subjected to the germination process
Germination influences the level of diastatic activity
Germination causes variation in protein content
Germination leads to a decrease in starch content
Germination leads to an increase in soluble sugars
Germination affects the tannin content in grains
Germination affects the total phenols content in grains
Sorghum cultivars exhibit varying levels of diastatic activity
Sorghum cultivars exhibit varying levels of protein content
